Issue Description: img element missing alt attribute entirely. Screen readers cannot convey meaning of the logo image.
Recommendation: Add descriptive alt attribute. If decorative, use alt="" and role="presentation".

Issue Description: Icon buttons contain only SVG with no accessible name. No aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible text on buttons.
Recommendation: Add aria-label to each button describing its action. Add aria-hidden="true" to SVG icons.

Issue Description: div element has onClick handler but no keyboard accessibility. Missing tabIndex, role, and onKeyDown handler.
Recommendation: Replace div with button element, or add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space.

Issue Description: Hardcoded id="card-item" produces duplicate IDs when multiple Cards rendered. Breaks assistive technology parsing.
Recommendation: Generate unique IDs using useId() or pass unique ID via props.

Issue Description: html lang="en" may not match actual content language. Screen readers will use incorrect pronunciation.
Recommendation: Set lang attribute to match primary content language (e.g., lang="ko" for Korean).

Issue Description: SVG icons lack title elements and aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may read SVG markup content.
Recommendation: Add aria-hidden="true" to decorative SVGs or add title element inside SVG for meaningful icons.

Issue Description: Page body lacks semantic landmark elements (main, nav, header, footer). No structural organization for assistive technology.
Recommendation: Wrap content in main, header, nav, footer elements to enable landmark navigation.

Issue Description: section element lacks accessible name. Landmark sections should have aria-label or aria-labelledby.
Recommendation: Add aria-labelledby referencing the h2 heading, or add aria-label to the section.

Issue Description: Link texts may not be sufficient out of context for screen reader users navigating via links list.
Recommendation: Ensure link text is descriptive in isolation. Add aria-label for more context if needed.

Issue Description: Clickable div has no ARIA role. Interactive elements must expose their role to assistive technologies.
Recommendation: Add role="button" to div, or preferably refactor to use native button element.

Issue Description: Dynamically rendered items use plain div instead of semantic list structure. Screen readers cannot announce count/position.
Recommendation: Use ul/li elements or role="list" and role="listitem" for the mapped items.

Issue Description: innerHTML with user input (location.hash) is XSS risk that could disrupt accessibility tree.
Recommendation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML to prevent injection.

Issue Description: Page title "Sample Page" is generic and non-descriptive.
Recommendation: Use descriptive unique page title identifying content and site.

Issue Description: Content injected via setTimeout without ARIA live region notification to assistive technologies.
Recommendation: Wrap dynamic content in aria-live="polite" region.

Issue Description: Clickable div has no visible focus indicator styles for keyboard navigation.
Recommendation: Add focus:ring or :focus-visible CSS styles for visible focus indicator.

Issue Description: No skip navigation link provided for keyboard users to bypass repeated content.
Recommendation: Add visually hidden skip link at top of page targeting main content area.

Issue Description: Dashboard could benefit from skip navigation mechanism as navigation grows.
Recommendation: Consider adding skip link. Existing landmarks provide some bypass for screen reader users.
